# Data Report

This report documents the AircraftVerse data used in the visualization product and explains how the raw design folders were transformed into the compact table used by the dashboard.

## Raw Data

: Overview of raw data used in the project. {#tbl-raw-overview}

| Name | Source | Storage location |
|---|---|---|
| AircraftVerse | Public AircraftVerse dataset from SRI / Zenodo, containing simulated battery-powered aircraft configurations | Raw zip files are downloaded locally to `data_raw/` and are not committed |

The original dataset is available on Zenodo at <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6525446>. The accompanying paper is available at <https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05562>.

AircraftVerse contains simulated aircraft designs with multiple data artefacts per design, including design trees, low-level design descriptions, 3D geometry files and simulation outputs. For this project, the relevant files are:

- `design_tree.json`: symbolic aircraft design tree with topology and component choices.
- `design_low_level.json`: expanded design representation.
- `output.json`: simulation output metrics such as mass, maximum distance, hover time and speed.

The original geometry files such as STL and STEP are very large and are not needed for the dashboard questions. They were excluded from the project data pipeline to keep the repository and deployment manageable.

## Processing Workflow

The processing workflow has two stages:

1. **Slim raw extraction**: `data_acquisition/prepare_slim_aircraftverse.py` extracts only the JSON files required for analysis from the three AircraftVerse zip files.
2. **Processed dashboard table**: `data_acquisition/build_processed_dataset.py` flattens the slim design folders into `data/aircraft_designs.csv.gz`.

The processed file is committed because it is compact and reproducible. The raw extraction remains local and ignored by Git.

Expected processed dataset:

- Rows: 27,714 aircraft designs
- Columns: 42 variables
- File size: about 2.6 MB compressed

## Processed Data Catalogue

: Main processed variables used in the dashboard. {#tbl-data-catalogue}

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `design_name` | categorical | AircraftVerse design folder identifier |
| `hub_type` | categorical | Raw generator name for the hub / layout family |
| `fuselage_type` | categorical | Single-battery or dual-battery fuselage family |
| `battery_type` | categorical | Battery component identifier |
| `battery_capacity_mah` | numeric | Battery capacity parsed from component name |
| `battery_cells_s` | numeric | Battery cell count parsed from component name |
| `motor_count` | numeric | Number of motors found in the design tree |
| `prop_count` | numeric | Number of propellers found in the design tree |
| `wing_count` | numeric | Number of wings found in the design tree |
| `arm_count` | numeric | Number of arm length values found in the design tree |
| `arm_length_mean_mm` | numeric | Mean arm length in millimetres |
| `wing_span_total_mm` | numeric | Sum of wing spans in millimetres |
| `mass_kg` | numeric | Simulated aircraft mass |
| `max_distance_m` | numeric | Simulated maximum flight distance |
| `hover_time_s` | numeric | Simulated hover time |
| `max_speed_mps` | numeric | Simulated maximum speed |
| `interferences` | numeric | Number of geometry interferences reported by the simulation output |
| `can_hover` | boolean | True when hover time is greater than zero |
| `can_fly` | boolean | True when maximum distance or maximum speed is greater than zero |
| `distance_per_kg` | numeric | Maximum distance divided by mass |
| `hover_per_kg` | numeric | Hover time divided by mass |
| `mission_score` | numeric | Internal ranking score combining distance, hover time, speed, mass and interferences |

## Data Characteristics

: Summary of selected performance metrics. {#tbl-performance-summary}

| Metric | Mean | Median | 90th percentile | Maximum |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Mass (kg) | 50.02 | 6.21 | 14.87 | 240,029.31 |
| Maximum distance (m) | 1,210.53 | 0.00 | 4,007.32 | 40,904.94 |
| Hover time (s) | 49.23 | 0.00 | 169.91 | 1,306.54 |
| Maximum speed (m/s) | 9.18 | 0.00 | 30.00 | 50.00 |
| Interferences | 16.96 | 11.00 | 41.00 | 373.00 |

Important exploratory findings:

- 10,737 designs are forward-flight capable.
- 7,789 designs are hover-capable.
- Many designs have zero distance, zero hover time and zero speed. This makes viability a central analysis question.
- The dataset contains extreme outliers, especially in mass. The dashboard therefore uses percentile-clipped plotting columns for some distribution views, while preserving original values for tables and summaries.
- The processed data contains both categorical features, such as hub family and fuselage type, and numerical features, such as mass, distance and hover time. It satisfies the assignment requirements of more than 100 observations, more than 6 variables, and mixed variable types.

## Implications For Visualization

The data characteristics shaped the final dashboard design:

- Viability is shown explicitly because non-viable designs are common.
- Grouped comparisons by hub family, battery cell count, fuselage type and wing presence are more interpretable than inspecting thousands of individual designs.
- Median and 90th percentile values are used for family comparisons because maxima alone are too sensitive to outliers.
- Hover details and tables support exploration without overwhelming users with raw JSON structure.
